skill.name: changelog-generator
skill.description: Creates user-facing changelogs and release notes from git commit history by grouping changes, filtering internal noise, and rewriting technical commits into clear customer language.
skill.summary: Generate clear release notes from git commit history.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found no executable code, shell commands, crypto routines, network access, or secret handling in SKILL.md. The matches are fenced prompt examples and ordinary documentation text, so the skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Prompt Examples Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 35-65 contain fenced examples of user prompts and sample markdown output, not Ruby backtick execution or runnable shell commands. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: the only matched backticks delimit markdown code fences, and no command interpreter, subprocess call, or executable script is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Documentation Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 3, 26, 79, and 101 contain descriptive changelog text and no md5, sha1, hashing, encryption, signing, or authentication logic. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: review found no cryptographic API usage or security-sensitive transformation in the skill file.

content.value_statement: Release teams often spend hours turning technical commits into readable updates. This skill organizes git history into clear changelogs, release notes, and product update drafts.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes commit history for a selected period, version range, or release target.
Groups changes into categories such as features, improvements, fixes, security, and breaking changes.
Rewrites technical commit messages into user-facing release note language.
Filters routine internal work such as tests, refactors, and maintenance commits.
Formats output for changelogs, GitHub releases, app store notes, and product update pages.
Applies a project style guide when the user provides one.
limitations: Requires access to relevant git history or commit summaries to produce useful results.
Cannot verify product impact when commit messages are vague or incomplete.
Generated changelogs should be reviewed before publication.
Does not perform release management tasks such as tagging, publishing, or deployment.

use_cases.title: Prepare Public Release Notes
use_cases.description: Convert commits for a new version into concise release notes for customers and stakeholders.

use_cases.title: Maintain a Project Changelog
use_cases.description: Create structured changelog entries after each sprint or release cycle.

use_cases.title: Draft Update Announcements
use_cases.description: Summarize recent changes for email updates, app store submissions, or social posts.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Changelog
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a quick changelog from recent commits.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a user-facing changelog from commits since the last release. Group changes by type and keep the language clear.

prompt_templates.title: Date Range Summary
prompt_templates.scenario: Summarize work from a specific period.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate release notes for commits between March 1 and March 15. Exclude internal-only changes unless users would notice them.

prompt_templates.title: Versioned Release Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare notes for a named version.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create release notes for version 2.5.0 from the relevant git history. Highlight features, fixes, improvements, and breaking changes.

prompt_templates.title: Style-Guided Changelog
prompt_templates.scenario: Use a team style guide for advanced output control.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a changelog for commits since v2.4.0 using CHANGELOG_STYLE.md. Match the existing tone, headings, and level of detail.

output_examples.input: Commits from the past week include team workspaces, keyboard shortcuts, faster sync, better search, and image upload fixes.
output: New features: Team workspaces and keyboard shortcuts are presented as customer benefits.
Improvements: Sync and search updates are grouped under performance and usability.
Fixes: Upload and scheduling issues are listed in plain language.

output_examples.input: Release 2.5.0 includes API pagination changes, timezone fixes, and redesigned billing settings.
output: The generated notes separate billing improvements from developer-facing API changes.
Breaking changes are called out clearly before general fixes.
The timezone fix is rewritten as a user-visible reliability improvement.

output_examples.input: A team needs a monthly update for customers from mixed feature, fix, and maintenance commits.
output: The skill filters maintenance noise and keeps customer-visible updates.
The result is organized into features, improvements, fixes, and security sections.

best_practices: Run the skill from the repository root or provide the exact commit range.
Share product names, audience, and tone preferences before generating final copy.
Review the output with engineering and product owners before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated changelogs without checking technical accuracy.
Do not include internal refactors unless they create user-visible value.
Do not rely on vague commit messages without adding product context.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my repository?
faq.answer: No. The skill describes how to generate changelog text and does not include scripts that modify files.

faq.question: Can it create GitHub release notes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can format commit summaries into release notes suitable for GitHub releases.

faq.question: Can it follow my existing changelog style?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide your changelog guidelines or an existing changelog example for tone and structure.

faq.question: What input works best?
faq.answer: A clear date range, version range, or commit list produces the best changelog draft.

faq.question: Does it detect breaking changes automatically?
faq.answer: It can identify likely breaking changes from commit content, but teams should verify them manually.

faq.question: Is this only for customer-facing changelogs?
faq.answer: No. It can also create internal release summaries, product updates, and app store descriptions.
